title: Beauty never functions as consent
source_observation: Manimekalai is repeatedly described as beautiful and publicly visible while others act on their desire
risk: The camera could imply that adornment performance training or beauty invites pursuit
decision: Separate her appearance from others' entitlement and never frame beauty or visibility as consent
rationale: Protects character autonomy and prevents the film from reproducing the prince's assumption
required_review: Classical Tamil; gender studies; director; cinematography; intimacy coordinator

title: Udayakumaran's persistence is not romantic proof
source_observation: The prince continues pursuit despite Manimekalai's chosen direction
risk: Romance conventions could reward persistence and convert refusal into hidden desire
decision: Stage his longing seriously but treat continued pursuit as failure to respect present autonomy
rationale: Preserves tragedy without endorsing coercive romance
required_review: Screenplay; classical Tamil; gender review; performance

title: Courtesan remains a provisional translation category
source_observation: English translations use courtesan for Madhavi's and Chitrapathi's professional world
risk: Production shorthand could collapse distinct institutions across centuries
decision: Use the term only with an uncertainty note and approve no devadasi prostitution concubinage or caste equivalence before specialist review
required_review: Classical Tamil; performance history; gender and labour history; publicity

title: Three generations are competing strategies not moral types
source_observation: Madhavi Chitrapathi and Manimekalai make different choices around lineage livelihood grief religion and elite patronage
risk: One could become the liberated woman another the fallen woman and another the villainous elder
decision: Give each woman material reasons intellectual dignity and accountability while keeping their conflicts real
rationale: Creates intergenerational drama without moral caricature
required_review: Classical Tamil; gender history; performance history; screenplay

title: Female renunciation is chosen disciplined vocation
source_observation: Manimekalai and Madhavi turn toward ascetic life under different conditions
risk: Renunciation could be framed as disgust with sexuality or a simple escape from womanhood
decision: Show renunciation as disciplined reorganisation of obligation identity and service while retaining uncertainty about institutional status
rationale: Preserves spiritual agency without anti-body rhetoric
required_review: Buddhist studies; classical Tamil; monastic history; costume; actor preparation

title: Sudhamathi owns her survival narrative
source_observation: Sudhamathi recounts danger abandonment and family collapse and later acts as companion and strategist
risk: Her suffering could become exposition for the heroine or eroticised flashback violence
decision: Give her narrative time point of view aftermath judgement and continuing agency under trauma-informed protocols
required_review: Classical Tamil; trauma consultant; intimacy coordinator; screenplay

title: Female divine authority is differentiated and nondecorative
source_observation: Female divine figures transport warn instruct and transfer responsibility
risk: They could become interchangeable glowing helpers or maternal stereotypes
decision: Design each figure through distinct place knowledge speech timing and moral authority rather than sexualised spectacle
rationale: Keeps women as agents of cosmology and ethical change
required_review: Classical Tamil; religious history; production design; VFX; costume

title: Gendered disguises are serious strategies
source_observation: Manimekalai assumes Kayasandihai's form and later a sage or male form
risk: Transformation scenes could become comedy costume gimmick or publicity claims about modern identity categories
decision: Treat disguise as embodied access risk and ethical complication and avoid comic or anachronistic identity framing
rationale: Preserves the narrative function while protecting contemporary communities from false claims
required_review: Classical Tamil; gender studies; performance; VFX; sensitivity review

title: Women-to-women knowledge networks remain visible
source_observation: Women carry warnings resources family history political access training and survival strategies throughout the epic
risk: Compression could leave women speaking only about men or romantic desire
decision: Preserve consequential conversations and exchanges among women that alter action institutions and interpretation
rationale: Maintains the epic's distributed female agency
required_review: Screenplay; classical Tamil; editor; character department

title: Female agency includes accountability
source_observation: Chitrapathi Rajamadevi and other women may reproduce coercive systems while acting from grief dependence or status
risk: A gender-conscious adaptation could excuse harmful female actions or turn them into misogynistic proof
decision: Show motive constraint power consequence and accountability without denying grief or structural pressure
required_review: Gender studies; ethics; screenplay; actor preparation; trauma review
